authorities.name: California Department of Fish and Wildlife
U.S. Geological Survey (Geographic Names Information System)

sources.title: 14 CCR §7.40(b)(72) — Upper Penitencia Creek (Santa Clara Co.)
sources.quotedText: (72) Upper Penitencia Creek (Santa Clara Co.), a tributary to Coyote Creek. Closed to all fishing all year.

sources.title: Penitencia Creek — Santa Clara County Parks
California Fish and Game Code §7145 — Sport Fishing License Requirement
sources.quotedText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...

sources.title: USGS GNIS (The National Map Gazetteer) — Streams layer record for gaz_id 255184, "Upper Penitencia Creek", Stream, Santa Clara Co., CA
sources.quotedText: {"gaz_id":255184,"gaz_name":"Upper Penitencia Creek","gaz_featureclass":"Stream","fcode":46000,"state_alpha":"CA","county_name":"Santa Clara"}; geometry multipoint, mouth point (Web Mercator -13567646.9434, 4490448.4547) converts to lon -121.880246, lat 37.367445 — matching the independently-cited GNIS coordinate for this creek's confluence with Coyote Creek (37.3674405N, 121.8802331W per Wikidata GNIS 230484 for the same confluence). The file's previous coordinate (-121.7989, 37.3898) was ~7.6km away (near Alum Rock Park, upstream) and has been corrected to this mouth coordinate.

parameters.note: Closed to all fishing all year, on Upper Penitencia Creek, a tributary to Coyote Creek, running through Alum Rock Park in San Jose to its confluence with Coyote Creek near Berryessa Road.

regulations.humanSummary: Closed to all fishing all year
regulations.verbatimText: Closed to all fishing all year.

parameters.note: Anglers 16 years of age or older must have a valid California sport fishing license.

regulations.humanSummary: California sport fishing license required for anglers 16+
regulations.verbatimText: Except as otherwise provided in this article, every person 16 years of age or older who takes any fish, reptile, or amphibian for any purpose other than profit shall first obtain a valid license for that purpose...
